title: The Forest at Springtown's Edge
content: The forest at Springtown's edge supplies Taro with cedar, oak, and quiet perspective. He knows which fallen branches can become picture frames, which trunks should be left to nurse moss, and which trees mark old walking paths. Taro never cuts living wood without a reason and a replacement planting. To him, the forest is not a warehouse but a patient teacher. Many of his best carvings begin as walks where he listens more than he speaks.
